message:
feat(workflows/handlers): tick 214 HandleSessionEnd::execute_step_cx override — first per-impl Workflow migration (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

First per-impl Workflow migration of the ft-xbnl0.2.3 cx-first sweep.
Sets the pattern for the other 7+ Workflow impls in handlers.rs.

Since ft-xbnl0.2.2, the Workflow trait has had `execute_step_cx`
as a cx-first entry (traits.rs L123) with a default impl that
delegates to `execute_step`. Tick 187 routed the runner through
`execute_step_cx` when cx is Some; individual impls that want
fine-grained cancellation then need to override the default.

`HandleSessionEnd` is the simplest Workflow impl — 2 steps, only
3 storage calls, no wezterm dispatch. Ideal demonstrator.

Tick 214 adds an `execute_step_cx` override that:
1. Pre-step `cx.checkpoint()` gates entry so a cx-cancel between
   steps short-circuits before any storage work.
2. Routes all 3 storage calls through cx-first siblings:
   - `get_sessions_for_pane_with_cx(cx, pane_id)` (read)
   - `upsert_agent_session_with_cx(cx, record)` (write → send_with_cx
     mpsc backpressure path from tick 173)
   - `record_usage_metrics_batch_with_cx(cx, metrics)` (write)

Legacy `execute_step` is preserved unchanged for ambient-cx
callers. Only `execute_step_cx` is new.

Tracing annotation: `explicit_cx = true` field added to info/error
spans so operators can distinguish legacy vs cx-first code paths
in structured logs.

Next-impl migration order suggestion (simplest → most complex):
- HandleSessionStartContext — also session-lifecycle, similar shape
- HandleOnErrorCassSearch — cass subprocess + storage reads
- HandleSwarmLearningIndex — storage + event bus
- HandleAuthRequired / HandleClaudeCodeLimits / HandleGeminiQuota —
  approval + policy dispatch
- HandleProcessTriageLifecycle — most complex; multiple steps,
  plan evaluation, injection

Verification: 1168 / 1168 workflows:: tests pass under
--features asupersync-runtime via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang,
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
